speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: On Thursday, sir? The time is very short.

speaker: PARIS
text: My father Capulet will have it so,

speaker: PARIS
text: And I am nothing slow to slack his haste.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: You say you do not know the lady’s mind?

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Uneven is the course. I like it not.

speaker: PARIS
text: Immoderately she weeps for Tybalt’s death,

speaker: PARIS
text: And therefore have I little talk of love,

speaker: PARIS
text: For Venus smiles not in a house of tears.

speaker: PARIS
text: Now, sir, her father counts it dangerous

speaker: PARIS
text: That she do give her sorrow so much sway,

speaker: PARIS
text: And in his wisdom hastes our marriage

speaker: PARIS
text: To stop the inundation of her tears,

speaker: PARIS
text: Which, too much minded by herself alone,

speaker: PARIS
text: May be put from her by society.

speaker: PARIS
text: Now do you know the reason of this haste.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: I would I knew not why it should be slowed.—

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Look, sir, here comes the lady toward my cell.

speaker: PARIS
text: Happily met, my lady and my wife.

speaker: JULIET
text: That may be, sir, when I may be a wife.

speaker: PARIS
text: That “may be” must be, love, on Thursday next.

speaker: JULIET
text: What must be shall be.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: That’s a certain text.

speaker: PARIS
text: Come you to make confession to this father?

speaker: JULIET
text: To answer that, I should confess to you.

speaker: PARIS
text: Do not deny to him that you love me.

speaker: JULIET
text: I will confess to you that I love him.

speaker: PARIS
text: So will you, I am sure, that you love me.

speaker: JULIET
text: If I do so, it will be of more price

speaker: JULIET
text: Being spoke behind your back than to your face.

speaker: PARIS
text: Poor soul, thy face is much abused with tears.

speaker: JULIET
text: The tears have got small victory by that,

speaker: JULIET
text: For it was bad enough before their spite.

speaker: PARIS
text: Thou wrong’st it more than tears with that report.

speaker: JULIET
text: That is no slander, sir, which is a truth,

speaker: JULIET
text: And what I spake, I spake it to my face.

speaker: PARIS
text: Thy face is mine, and thou hast slandered it.

speaker: JULIET
text: It may be so, for it is not mine own.—

speaker: JULIET
text: Are you at leisure, holy father, now,

speaker: JULIET
text: Or shall I come to you at evening Mass?

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: My leisure serves me, pensive daughter, now.—

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: My lord, we must entreat the time alone.

speaker: PARIS
text: God shield I should disturb devotion!—

speaker: PARIS
text: Juliet, on Thursday early will I rouse you.

speaker: PARIS
text: Till then, adieu, and keep this holy kiss.

speaker: JULIET
text: O, shut the door, and when thou hast done so,

speaker: JULIET
text: Come weep with me, past hope, past care, past help.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: O Juliet, I already know thy grief.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: It strains me past the compass of my wits.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: I hear thou must, and nothing may prorogue it,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: On Thursday next be married to this County.

speaker: JULIET
text: Tell me not, friar, that thou hearest of this,

speaker: JULIET
text: Unless thou tell me how I may prevent it.

speaker: JULIET
text: If in thy wisdom thou canst give no help,

speaker: JULIET
text: Do thou but call my resolution wise,

speaker: JULIET
text: And with this knife I’ll help it presently.

speaker: JULIET
text: God joined my heart and Romeo’s, thou our hands;

speaker: JULIET
text: And ere this hand, by thee to Romeo’s sealed,

speaker: JULIET
text: Shall be the label to another deed,

speaker: JULIET
text: Or my true heart with treacherous revolt

speaker: JULIET
text: Turn to another, this shall slay them both.

speaker: JULIET
text: Therefore out of thy long-experienced time

speaker: JULIET
text: Give me some present counsel, or, behold,

speaker: JULIET
text: ’Twixt my extremes and me this bloody knife

speaker: JULIET
text: Shall play the umpire, arbitrating that

speaker: JULIET
text: Which the commission of thy years and art

speaker: JULIET
text: Could to no issue of true honor bring.

speaker: JULIET
text: Be not so long to speak. I long to die

speaker: JULIET
text: If what thou speak’st speak not of remedy.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Hold, daughter, I do spy a kind of hope,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Which craves as desperate an execution

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: As that is desperate which we would prevent.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: If, rather than to marry County Paris,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Thou hast the strength of will to slay thyself,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Then is it likely thou wilt undertake

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: A thing like death to chide away this shame,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: That cop’st with death himself to ’scape from it;

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And if thou darest, I’ll give thee remedy.

speaker: JULIET
text: O, bid me leap, rather than marry Paris,

speaker: JULIET
text: From off the battlements of any tower,

speaker: JULIET
text: Or walk in thievish ways, or bid me lurk

speaker: JULIET
text: Where serpents are. Chain me with roaring bears,

speaker: JULIET
text: Or hide me nightly in a charnel house,

speaker: JULIET
text: O’ercovered quite with dead men’s rattling bones,

speaker: JULIET
text: With reeky shanks and yellow chapless skulls.

speaker: JULIET
text: Or bid me go into a new-made grave

speaker: JULIET
text: And hide me with a dead man in his shroud

speaker: JULIET
text: (Things that to hear them told have made me tremble),

speaker: JULIET
text: And I will do it without fear or doubt,

speaker: JULIET
text: To live an unstained wife to my sweet love.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Hold, then. Go home; be merry; give consent

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: To marry Paris. Wednesday is tomorrow.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Tomorrow night look that thou lie alone;

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Let not the Nurse lie with thee in thy chamber.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Take thou this vial, being then in bed,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And this distilling liquor drink thou off;

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: When presently through all thy veins shall run

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: A cold and drowsy humor; for no pulse

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Shall keep his native progress, but surcease.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: No warmth, no breath shall testify thou livest.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: The roses in thy lips and cheeks shall fade

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: To paly ashes, thy eyes’ windows fall

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Like death when he shuts up the day of life.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Each part, deprived of supple government,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Shall, stiff and stark and cold, appear like death,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And in this borrowed likeness of shrunk death

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Thou shalt continue two and forty hours

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And then awake as from a pleasant sleep.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Now, when the bridegroom in the morning comes

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: To rouse thee from thy bed, there art thou dead.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Then, as the manner of our country is,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: In thy best robes uncovered on the bier

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Thou shalt be borne to that same ancient vault

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Where all the kindred of the Capulets lie.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: In the meantime, against thou shalt awake,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Shall Romeo by my letters know our drift,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And hither shall he come, and he and I

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Will watch thy waking, and that very night

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Shall Romeo bear thee hence to Mantua.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And this shall free thee from this present shame,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: If no inconstant toy nor womanish fear

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Abate thy valor in the acting it.

speaker: JULIET
text: Give me, give me! O, tell not me of fear!

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Hold, get you gone. Be strong and prosperous

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: In this resolve. I’ll send a friar with speed

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: To Mantua with my letters to thy lord.

speaker: JULIET
text: Love give me strength, and strength shall help afford.

speaker: JULIET
text: Farewell, dear father.
